title: verbascum simplex subsp. valentinum
intro: Verbascum simplex subsp. valentinum, also known as Valentia mullein, is a subspecies of Verbascum simplex, a biennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in saponins and flavonoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, and is believed to help soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Verbascum simplex subsp. valentinum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
